 {"id":"wa-20fw","title":"Create WezTerm fork with wa feature flag and minimal integration points","description":"## Overview

Fork WezTerm and add the minimal code needed to support wa event delivery. All changes are behind \`#[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]\`.

## Prerequisites

- Completed: wa-3gon (WaEventSink trait design)

## Repository Setup

### 1. Fork WezTerm
\`\`\`bash
gh repo fork wez/wezterm --clone
cd wezterm
git checkout -b feature/wa-integration
\`\`\`

### 2. Add Feature Flag
In Cargo.toml (workspace root):
\`\`\`toml
[workspace.features]
wa-integration = []
\`\`\`

In relevant crates (mux/Cargo.toml, wezterm/Cargo.toml):
\`\`\`toml
[features]
wa-integration = []
\`\`\`

## Modification Points

### 1. mux/src/lib.rs — Event Sink Registration

\`\`\`rust
#[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
mod wa_events;

#[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
use wa_events::WaEventSink;

#[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
static WA_EVENT_SINK: OnceCell<Arc<dyn WaEventSink>> = OnceCell::new();

#[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
pub fn register_wa_event_sink(sink: Arc<dyn WaEventSink>) {
    WA_EVENT_SINK.set(sink).ok();
}

#[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
pub(crate) fn emit_wa_event(event: impl FnOnce(&dyn WaEventSink)) {
    if let Some(sink) = WA_EVENT_SINK.get() {
        event(sink.as_ref());
    }
}
\`\`\`

### 2. mux/src/pane.rs — Emit Output Events

In the method that receives PTY output:
\`\`\`rust
fn process_pty_output(&mut self, data: &[u8]) {
    // Existing WezTerm code...
    
    #[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
    crate::emit_wa_event(|sink| {
        sink.on_pane_output(self.pane_id, data);
    });
}
\`\`\`

### 3. mux/src/pane.rs — Emit State Changes

In methods that update pane state:
\`\`\`rust
fn set_title(&mut self, title: String) {
    // Existing code...
    
    #[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
    self.emit_state_change();
}

#[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
fn emit_state_change(&self) {
    crate::emit_wa_event(|sink| {
        sink.on_pane_state_change(self.pane_id, &self.get_wa_state());
    });
}
\`\`\`

### 4. mux/src/pane.rs — Emit User-Var Changes

In the OSC 1337 handler:
\`\`\`rust
fn set_user_var(&mut self, name: String, value: String) {
    // Existing code...
    
    #[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
    crate::emit_wa_event(|sink| {
        sink.on_user_var_changed(self.pane_id, &name, &value);
    });
}
\`\`\`

### 5. wezterm/src/main.rs — Initialize Sink

\`\`\`rust
#[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
fn init_wa_integration() {
    if let Ok(socket_path) = std::env::var("WEZTERM_WA_SOCKET") {
        match wa_socket_sink::connect(&socket_path) {
            Ok(sink) => mux::register_wa_event_sink(sink),
            Err(e) => log::warn!("Failed to connect to wa socket: {}", e),
        }
    }
}
\`\`\`

## Testing the Fork

### 1. Build with Feature
\`\`\`bash
cargo build --features wa-integration
\`\`\`

### 2. Verify Default Build Unaffected
\`\`\`bash
cargo build  # Should work identically to upstream
\`\`\`

### 3. Integration Test
\`\`\`bash
# Start wa watch (which creates the socket)
wa watch &

# Start WezTerm with wa integration
WEZTERM_WA_SOCKET=/tmp/wa/events.sock ./target/debug/wezterm

# Verify events received
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] WezTerm fork created with feature/wa-integration branch
- [ ] Feature flag added to workspace
- [ ] Event emission added to pane output path
- [ ] Event emission added to state change paths
- [ ] Event emission added to user-var handler
- [ ] Default build (no feature) compiles and runs unchanged
- [ ] Feature build compiles and runs
- [ ] Events actually received by wa (integration test)

## Risks

- WezTerm internal APIs may change — minimize touchpoints
- Performance regression — benchmark before/after
- Thread safety bugs — careful with event emission

## Files to Modify (in WezTerm fork)

- Cargo.toml (workspace) — add feature
- mux/Cargo.toml — add feature
- mux/src/lib.rs — event sink registration
- mux/src/pane.rs — event emission
- wezterm/Cargo.toml — add feature
- wezterm/src/main.rs — initialization
- NEW: mux/src/wa_events.rs — trait + socket sink implementation

## References

- WezTerm mux: https://github.com/wez/wezterm/tree/main/mux
- Rust feature flags: https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/reference/features.html","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:49:14.103113431Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T06:52:05.65303Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T06:52:05.653011Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-20fw","depends_on_id":"wa-2xe4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:21Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-20gu","title":"FTUI-05.2.a Home view parity checklist and intentional delta ledger","description":"## Background
Home view migration needs explicit parity tracking to avoid silent UX regressions during frankentui adoption.

## Deliverables
- widget-by-widget parity checklist (layout, indicators, actions, refresh behavior)
- intentional-delta ledger with justification and user-impact notes
- evidence links to snapshots and test outputs for each checklist item

## Acceptance Criteria
- every Home widget state has either parity confirmation or approved intentional delta
- checklist references deterministic snapshot and unit-test artifacts
- failure notes include reproducible steps and relevant logs for debugging.,","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:14:16.495001658Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-09T04:33:12.149218009Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T04:33:12.14908932Z","close_reason":"Wrote evidence/ftui-05.2/matrix.md with 16-row parity matrix: 14 pass, 1 intentional-delta (D4: layout strategy), 0 fail, 0 untested. Covers title, health badge, system status (4 indicators), metrics (pane/event/unhandled/triage counts), loading state, quick help, zero-height safety, minimum-height rendering, and manual refresh lifecycle.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-20gu","depends_on_id":"wa-3pc9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:14:16.516338961Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"},{"issue_id":"wa-20gu","depends_on_id":"wa-2i6m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:24:59.74365177Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"}]}
 {"id":"wa-21cz","title":"FTUI-03.3 Implement inline-mode strategy and alt-screen transition policy","description":"## Background\nwa operators rely on stable terminal behavior. We need deterministic policy for inline and alternate screen transitions during interactive flows.\n\n## Deliverables\n- mode policy by command context (UI run, action command, return)\n- scrollback safety rules\n- explicit transition hooks and error handling\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- mode transitions are deterministic and documented\n- no terminal corruption in repeated mode switches.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LavenderGrove","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:07:53.672502273Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-09T01:54:30.422232509Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T01:54:30.4221685Z","close_reason":"done","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-21cz","depends_on_id":"wa-1brb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:07:53.68571839Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"},{"issue_id":"wa-21cz","depends_on_id":"wa-2qyt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:16:29.309869498Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"}]}
-{"id":"wa-22o1q","title":"Zellij architectural inventory — module map, dependencies, key abstractions","description":"# Zellij Architectural Inventory

## Goal
Map Zellij's codebase structure to understand the high-level architecture before diving into specific areas.

## What to document

### Crate/module structure
- Map all workspace crates and their roles
- Identify the server binary vs client binary vs shared libraries
- Document the plugin host crate and WASM runtime integration

### Key abstractions
- How does Zellij model a Session, Tab, Pane, Layout?
- What traits/interfaces define the pane abstraction?
- How does the plugin system hook into the pane lifecycle?

### Dependency analysis
- What async runtime does Zellij use? (tokio? async-std? custom?)
- What serialization format for IPC? (protobuf? bincode? custom?)
- What WASM runtime? (wasmtime? wasmer?)
- Key performance-relevant dependencies (crossbeam, parking_lot, etc.)

### Build and feature flags
- What feature flags exist and what do they gate?
- How is the project structured for cross-platform support?

## Deliverable
A structured inventory document (in bead comments or a markdown file) that serves as a map for all subsequent analysis beads.

## Acceptance criteria
- All workspace crates documented with roles
- Key abstractions identified (Session, Tab, Pane, Layout, Plugin)
- Dependency tree analyzed for async runtime, IPC, WASM, and perf libs
- Clear map for navigating the codebase in subsequent beads

## Cross-references
- **wa-3bja.1** (Ghostty architectural inventory): Use the same methodology — module mapping, dependency analysis, key abstractions — for consistency across both competitor analyses. This enables the synthesis beads (wa-2bai5 and wa-3bja.5) to produce an apples-to-apples comparison.

## Notes
- The inventory structure (crate/module map, dependency analysis, key abstractions) deliberately mirrors wa-3bja.1's structure so both competitor analyses follow the same template. This consistency is critical for the unified roadmap comparison.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T15:53:29.010083Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-10T19:42:04.998343Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-22o1q","depends_on_id":"wa-okyhm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T15:54:56.440277Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"wa-22x4r","title":"Port existing async tests to LabRuntime","description":"# Port existing async tests to LabRuntime

## Background
Existing tests use #[tokio::test]. Port them to LabRuntime for deterministic, fast execution.

## Migration pattern
```rust
// Before
#[tokio::test]
async fn test_pool_acquire() {
    let pool = Pool::new(config);
    let conn = pool.acquire().await.unwrap();
    assert!(conn.is_healthy());
}

// After
#[test]
fn test_pool_acquire() {
    run_lab_test(42, "test_pool_acquire", |cx| async move {
        let pool = Pool::new(config);
        let conn = pool.acquire(cx).await.ok()?;
        assert!(conn.is_healthy());
        Outcome::ok(())
    });
}
```

## Port checklist
For EACH existing #[tokio::test]:
1. Convert to #[test] + run_lab_test()
2. Add Cx parameter to all async calls
3. Replace Result with Outcome where needed
4. Add seed parameter (start with 42, vary if flaky)
5. Add structured logging spans for key operations
6. Add oracle assertions (obligation_leak, task_leak)

## Logging requirements
Every ported test should log:
- Test start with seed and name
- Key async operations (acquire, send, recv, connect)
- State transitions (pool stats, channel state)
- Test completion with duration and outcome

## Benefits
- No flaky timing-dependent tests
- Reproducible failures (seed-locked)
- Fast execution (virtual time, no real sleeps)
- Schedule exploration can catch latent race conditions

## Acceptance criteria
- All existing #[tokio::test] ported to LabRuntime
- No #[tokio::test] remaining in codebase
- All tests pass with deterministic scheduling
- Test suite runs faster due to virtual time
- Every test has structured logging
- Every test has oracle assertions

## Cross-references
- See wa-a4goc (Phase 6: Build LabRuntime test infrastructure) — this bead depends on the run_lab_test(), run_chaos_test(), and run_exploration_test() helpers built there. All test fixtures (mock UnixStream, mock DirectMuxClient, mock Pool) come from wa-a4goc's tests/common/fixtures.rs.

## Benchmark requirements
- **Criterion benchmarks for test execution time comparison**: Add `benches/test_execution.rs` measuring:
  - Representative test suite execution time: LabRuntime vs #[tokio::test] for the same test logic
  - Per-test overhead: LabRuntime setup + oracle checks vs tokio::test macro expansion overhead
  - Virtual time acceleration factor: how many seconds of simulated time per millisecond of wall-clock time
  - Schedule exploration cost: DPOR overhead for a representative concurrent test
  - Target: LabRuntime tests should be at least 2x faster than equivalent tokio::test tests for time-dependent tests (due to virtual time eliminating real sleeps).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:51:07.4554Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-10T19:51:34.940886Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-22x4r","depends_on_id":"wa-a4goc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:52:02.983367Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-22x4r","depends_on_id":"wa-2h5wv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:52:03.093055Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-22x4r","depends_on_id":"wa-1m7nk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:52:03.200565Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-22x4r","depends_on_id":"wa-16hou","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:52:03.293569Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-22x4r","depends_on_id":"wa-k0tk5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T05:19:00.655047Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-22x4r","depends_on_id":"wa-124z4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T05:19:00.74727Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}

-{"id":"wa-2zywl","title":"Build and integrate process_triage — automated swarm process management","description":"# Build and Integrate process_triage

## Skills: /alien-artifact-coding (safety-critical), /extreme-software-optimization, /idea-wizard

## What process_triage should do
Automated, policy-driven process management for agent swarms. The system-performance-remediation skill's kill hierarchy, codified as a Rust library and deeply integrated into FrankenTerm.

## If process_triage already exists in /dp
Import as library dependency and integrate. If not, build it from the remediation skill's patterns.

## Kill hierarchy (from /system-performance-remediation)
| Priority | Category | Detection | Action | Risk |
|----------|----------|-----------|--------|------|
| 1 | Zombies | Z state | SIGCHLD to parent | Zero |
| 2 | Stuck tests | cargo test 12+ hours, <1% CPU | SIGTERM → SIGKILL | Low |
| 3 | Stuck CLIs | git/vercel 5+ min | SIGTERM | Low |
| 4 | Duplicate builds | Multiple cargo targets | Kill older, keep newest | Low |
| 5 | Abandoned servers | next dev/bun 24+ hours idle | SIGTERM | Low |
| 6 | Stale sessions | test tmux sessions | Direct kill | Medium |
| 7 | Confused agents | claude/codex 16+ hours | Kill parent, not children | Medium |
| 8 | Active agents | <16 hours, working | Protect or renice | High |
| 9 | System processes | systemd, sshd, postgres | NEVER TOUCH | Forbidden |

## Components

### 1. ProcessClassifier
Classify every process in the pane's process tree:
- PID, PPID, age, CPU%, memory, command, state
- Map to kill hierarchy category
- Output: ordered list of actions

### 2. TriageEngine
Execute actions in priority order:
- Dry-run mode: log what would happen
- Interactive mode: human approves each action
- Auto mode: execute non-destructive actions (zombies, stuck tests) automatically, queue medium-risk for approval

### 3. WhackAMoleDetector
Detect and break respawn cycles:
- Track kill→respawn patterns per process tree
- If child respawns within 5s of kill 3 times: kill the parent agent
- Formally verify this logic (/alien-artifact-coding)

### 4. Integration with FrankenTerm
- Runs as background task within FrankenTerm
- Pane-aware: knows which processes belong to which pane
- Triggers automatically on CPU pressure > 30%
- Manual trigger via CLI: wa triage [--dry-run]
- MCP tool: agents can request triage of their own pane

## Formal requirements (/alien-artifact-coding)
- ProcessClassifier MUST never misclassify a system process as killable
- TriageEngine MUST respect the priority ordering (never kill category 8 before category 2)
- WhackAMoleDetector MUST not false-positive on intentional restarts

## Tests
- Test classification of 20+ process scenarios
- Test kill hierarchy ordering
- Test whack-a-mole detection with mock process trees
- Test system process protection (NEVER kill sshd, mux server)
- Chaos test: random process creation/death, verify correct triage

## Acceptance criteria
- Process classification matches hierarchy table
- Triage executes in correct priority order
- Whack-a-mole detected and broken
- System processes NEVER touched
- Formally verified safety properties
- <500ms full triage scan for 2000+ processes

## macOS Compatibility
On macOS, `/proc` is not available for process enumeration. Use platform-specific alternatives:
- **Process enumeration**: `ps -o pid,ppid,args,state,%cpu,%mem,etime` instead of reading `/proc/[pid]/stat` and `/proc/[pid]/cmdline`
- **Process tree discovery**: `proc_pidinfo()` / `libproc.h` APIs (via the `libproc` crate) for per-process details, or fall back to `sysctl` KERN_PROC for the full process table
- **Zombie detection**: `ps -o state` reports `Z` on both platforms; no `/proc` needed
- **CPU/memory usage**: `proc_pid_rusage()` on macOS vs `/proc/[pid]/stat` on Linux
- Gate platform-specific code behind `#[cfg(target_os = "...")]` with a shared `ProcessInfo` abstraction

## Test Framework Requirements
- **Criterion benchmarks**: Benchmark triage decision latency — full classification of 2000 processes must complete in <500ms. Benchmark individual ProcessClassifier.classify() calls targeting <50μs per process. Include benchmark groups for varying process counts (100, 500, 1000, 2000).
- **Proptest**: Property-based tests for kill hierarchy correctness:
  - For any random set of processes, triage MUST process them in priority order (category 1 before 2, 2 before 3, etc.)
  - For any random process tree, system processes (category 9) are NEVER in the kill list
  - For any random kill/respawn sequence, WhackAMoleDetector correctly identifies respawn cycles (3+ respawns within 15s)

## Cross-References
- **wa-1ta8j** (Proactive pane lifecycle): process_triage provides the kill hierarchy that pane lifecycle management uses to decide when to reap zombies, detect stuck processes, and perform age-based cleanup. The two systems share process classification data.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-10T16:13:34.175116Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-10T23:22:39.144751-05:00"}
+{"id":"wa-2zywl","title":"Build and integrate process_triage — automated swarm process management","description":"# Build and Integrate process_triage

## Skills: /alien-artifact-coding (safety-critical), /extreme-software-optimization, /idea-wizard

## What process_triage should do
Automated, policy-driven process management for agent swarms. The system-performance-remediation skill's kill hierarchy, codified as a Rust library and deeply integrated into FrankenTerm.

## If process_triage already exists in /dp
Import as library dependency and integrate. If not, build it from the remediation skill's patterns.

## Kill hierarchy (from /system-performance-remediation)
| Priority | Category | Detection | Action | Risk |
|----------|----------|-----------|--------|------|
| 1 | Zombies | Z state | SIGCHLD to parent | Zero |
| 2 | Stuck tests | cargo test 12+ hours, <1% CPU | SIGTERM → SIGKILL | Low |
| 3 | Stuck CLIs | git/vercel 5+ min | SIGTERM | Low |
| 4 | Duplicate builds | Multiple cargo targets | Kill older, keep newest | Low |
| 5 | Abandoned servers | next dev/bun 24+ hours idle | SIGTERM | Low |
| 6 | Stale sessions | test tmux sessions | Direct kill | Medium |
| 7 | Confused agents | claude/codex 16+ hours | Kill parent, not children | Medium |
| 8 | Active agents | <16 hours, working | Protect or renice | High |
| 9 | System processes | systemd, sshd, postgres | NEVER TOUCH | Forbidden |

## Components

### 1. ProcessClassifier
Classify every process in the pane's process tree:
- PID, PPID, age, CPU%, memory, command, state
- Map to kill hierarchy category
- Output: ordered list of actions

### 2. TriageEngine
Execute actions in priority order:
- Dry-run mode: log what would happen
- Interactive mode: human approves each action
- Auto mode: execute non-destructive actions (zombies, stuck tests) automatically, queue medium-risk for approval

### 3. WhackAMoleDetector
Detect and break respawn cycles:
- Track kill→respawn patterns per process tree
- If child respawns within 5s of kill 3 times: kill the parent agent
- Formally verify this logic (/alien-artifact-coding)

### 4. Integration with FrankenTerm
- Runs as background task within FrankenTerm
- Pane-aware: knows which processes belong to which pane
- Triggers automatically on CPU pressure > 30%
- Manual trigger via CLI: wa triage [--dry-run]
- MCP tool: agents can request triage of their own pane

## Formal requirements (/alien-artifact-coding)
- ProcessClassifier MUST never misclassify a system process as killable
- TriageEngine MUST respect the priority ordering (never kill category 8 before category 2)
- WhackAMoleDetector MUST not false-positive on intentional restarts

## Tests
- Test classification of 20+ process scenarios
- Test kill hierarchy ordering
- Test whack-a-mole detection with mock process trees
- Test system process protection (NEVER kill sshd, mux server)
- Chaos test: random process creation/death, verify correct triage

## Acceptance criteria
- Process classification matches hierarchy table
- Triage executes in correct priority order
- Whack-a-mole detected and broken
- System processes NEVER touched
- Formally verified safety properties
- <500ms full triage scan for 2000+ processes

## macOS Compatibility
On macOS, `/proc` is not available for process enumeration. Use platform-specific alternatives:
- **Process enumeration**: `ps -o pid,ppid,args,state,%cpu,%mem,etime` instead of reading `/proc/[pid]/stat` and `/proc/[pid]/cmdline`
- **Process tree discovery**: `proc_pidinfo()` / `libproc.h` APIs (via the `libproc` crate) for per-process details, or fall back to `sysctl` KERN_PROC for the full process table
- **Zombie detection**: `ps -o state` reports `Z` on both platforms; no `/proc` needed
- **CPU/memory usage**: `proc_pid_rusage()` on macOS vs `/proc/[pid]/stat` on Linux
- Gate platform-specific code behind `#[cfg(target_os = "...")]` with a shared `ProcessInfo` abstraction

## Test Framework Requirements
- **Criterion benchmarks**: Benchmark triage decision latency — full classification of 2000 processes must complete in <500ms. Benchmark individual ProcessClassifier.classify() calls targeting <50μs per process. Include benchmark groups for varying process counts (100, 500, 1000, 2000).
- **Proptest**: Property-based tests for kill hierarchy correctness:
  - For any random set of processes, triage MUST process them in priority order (category 1 before 2, 2 before 3, etc.)
  - For any random process tree, system processes (category 9) are NEVER in the kill list
  - For any random kill/respawn sequence, WhackAMoleDetector correctly identifies respawn cycles (3+ respawns within 15s)

## Cross-References
- **wa-1ta8j** (Proactive pane lifecycle): process_triage provides the kill hierarchy that pane lifecycle management uses to decide when to reap zombies, detect stuck processes, and perform age-based cleanup. The two systems share process classification data.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-10T16:13:34.175116Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-10T23:28:07.883791-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-10T23:28:07.883791-05:00","close_reason":"Process triage engine implemented: TriageCategory enum (9 categories), ProcessClassifier with kill hierarchy, TriageEngine with plan builder, integration with process_tree module. 25 tests passing."}

 {"id":"wa-32z7","title":"Process re-launch engine — restart shells and agent processes in restored panes","description":"## Goal
Implement the process re-launch engine that restarts shells and AI agent processes in restored panes, using the process information captured in the MuxSnapshot.

## CRITICAL LIMITATION: Agent State is NOT Restored
**Re-launching an agent process (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini) starts a NEW session.** The agent in-memory state -- conversation history, context window, in-flight operations, tool results, working memory -- is permanently lost. Users MUST understand:
- Claude Code will start fresh with no knowledge of prior conversation
- Codex will lose its execution context and any pending operations
- Gemini will start a new session with default settings
- Any in-flight file edits, git operations, or multi-step tasks will be abandoned

This engine restores the PROCESS (correct binary, correct working directory, correct environment) but NOT the agent MIND. For true agent continuity, agents themselves must implement checkpoint/restore (out of scope for wa).

The primary value of process re-launch is:
1. **Shell restoration**: cd to correct working directory, restore shell environment
2. **Convenience**: User does not have to manually re-type launch commands for 50 panes
3. **Agent re-launch as opt-in shortcut**: Gets the agent running again, even if stateless

## Background and Motivation
After layout restoration and scrollback injection, panes have the right visual appearance but are running default shells (from the mux server spawn config). The original processes need to be re-launched.

This is the most complex restoration step because:
1. Not all processes CAN be restarted (some have ephemeral state)
2. Agent CLIs need specific arguments and environment variables
3. Working directories must be correct
4. Some processes need to be started in specific order (e.g., build watcher before agent)

## Technical Design

### Struct Definition
```rust
// Location: crates/wa-core/src/snapshot/restore_process.rs
pub struct ProcessLauncher {
    mux_client: Arc<DirectMuxClient>,
    config: LaunchConfig,
}

pub struct LaunchConfig {
    pub auto_launch: bool,         // default: false (require explicit opt-in)
    pub launch_shells: bool,       // default: true
    pub launch_agents: bool,       // default: false (safety: require explicit)
    pub launch_delay_ms: u64,      // default: 500 (stagger launches)
    pub agent_launch_commands: HashMap<String, String>,  // agent_type -> command template
}

pub struct ProcessPlan {
    pub pane_id: PaneId,
    pub original: ProcessInfo,
    pub action: LaunchAction,
    pub state_warning: Option<String>,  // Warning about state loss
}

pub enum LaunchAction {
    LaunchShell { shell: String, cwd: PathBuf },
    LaunchAgent { command: String, cwd: PathBuf, env: HashMap<String, String> },
    Skip { reason: String },
    Manual { hint: String },  // Tell user what was running
}
```

### Core Algorithm
1. **Plan phase** (dry-run safe, ALWAYS shown first):
   - For each pane in the snapshot, resolve the ProcessInfo
   - Determine LaunchAction based on process type and config
   - Shell processes -> LaunchShell
   - Known agent types -> LaunchAgent if opt-in enabled, else Manual with state loss warning
   - Unknown processes -> Manual with hint
   - Agent entries always include state_warning about context loss
   - Return ProcessPlan for review

2. **Execute phase** (requires explicit confirmation):
   - For each LaunchShell: send cd and exec shell to pane
   - For each LaunchAgent: send the configured launch command
   - Stagger launches by launch_delay_ms to prevent resource spikes
   - Track success/failure per pane

3. **Agent launch commands** (configurable in wa.toml):
```toml
[snapshots.agent_commands]
claude_code = "cd {cwd} && claude"
codex = "cd {cwd} && codex"
gemini = "cd {cwd} && gemini-cli"
```

### Plan Mode Output (wa snapshot restore --dry-run)
```
Pane  Original Process     Action         Warning
--------------------------------------------------------------
  0   bash @ /project      LaunchShell
  1   claude @ /project    Manual         Agent state will NOT be restored
  2   codex @ /other       Manual         Agent state will NOT be restored
  3   vim @ /project       Manual         Interactive program - manual restart needed
  4   bash @ /home         LaunchShell

Agents require --launch-agents flag. Agent processes start as NEW sessions
(conversation history, context, and in-flight work are lost).
```

### Safety Design
- By default, only shells are auto-launched (not agents)
- Agent re-launch requires explicit --launch-agents flag
- Plan mode is ALWAYS shown before execution (even with --launch-agents)
- Agent plans include prominent warning about state loss
- wa robot interface: plan returns JSON for programmatic review
- Each launch is logged with full command for audit trail

## Integration Points
- **PaneIdMap** from layout restoration: Maps to correct restored panes
- **ProcessInfo** from snapshot (bd-cuz schema): Original process details
- **AgentSessionRef** from snapshot: Links to wa agent session tracking
- **DirectMuxClient**: SendKeyStroke/SendPaste PDU for sending commands to panes
- **wa.toml config**: Agent launch command templates

## Key Files to Create/Modify
- CREATE: crates/wa-core/src/snapshot/restore_process.rs
- MODIFY: crates/wa-core/src/config.rs (add agent_commands config)
- MODIFY: crates/wa-core/src/snapshot/mod.rs

## Edge Cases
- Agent processes that need API keys in environment: use existing env or prompt
- Processes with file locks (e.g., cargo build): may fail to restart if lock exists
- Panes running interactive programs (vim, htop): skip with Manual hint
- Multiple agents in same working directory: stagger to avoid conflicts

## Dependencies
- bd-cuz: MuxSnapshot schema (ProcessInfo, AgentSessionRef)
- wa-e6pq: Layout restoration engine (provides PaneIdMap, panes must exist first)
- wa-1xcz: Scrollback injection (should complete before re-launch for visual continuity)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Shell processes re-launched in correct working directories
- Agent processes re-launched with correct commands (when opt-in enabled)
- Plan mode ALWAYS shows what would be launched, including state loss warnings
- Staggered launches prevent resource spikes
- Unknown processes reported as Manual with helpful hints
- Full audit trail of all launched commands
- Graceful handling of launch failures (continue with remaining panes)
- Agent state loss warning prominently displayed in plan output

## Estimated Effort
3-4 hours implementation, 1-2 hours testing

## macOS Compatibility
On macOS, the /proc/pid/cmdline filesystem does not exist. For recovering the original command line of a process during snapshot capture, use ps or sysctl instead:
```rust
#[cfg(target_os = "macos")]
fn get_process_cmdline(pid: i32) -> Result<Vec<String>> {
    // Use ps -o args= -p <pid> for command recovery
    let output = Command::new("ps")
        .args(&["-o", "args=", "-p", &pid.to_string()])
        .output()?;
    let cmdline = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout)
        .trim().to_string();
    Ok(shell_words::split(&cmdline)?)
}

#[cfg(target_os = "linux")]
fn get_process_cmdline(pid: i32) -> Result<Vec<String>> {
    let data = std::fs::read(format!("/proc/{}/cmdline", pid))?;
    Ok(data.split(|&b| b == 0)
        .filter(|s| !s.is_empty())
        .map(|s| String::from_utf8_lossy(s).to_string())
        .collect())
}
```
Note: ps -o args= may truncate long command lines. For full fidelity on macOS, also try sysctl kern.procargs2 via the sysctl crate as a fallback. Add shell_words to Cargo.toml for parsing the ps output.

## Test Requirements

### Criterion Benchmarks
Add benchmarks in crates/wa-core/benches/process_relaunch.rs:
- bench_plan_generation_50_panes: Generate ProcessPlan for 50 panes with mixed process types, target <10ms
- bench_shell_relaunch_latency: Measure time from launch command sent to shell prompt ready (single pane), target <500ms
- bench_staggered_launch_50_panes: Full staggered launch of 50 shell processes, measure wall-clock time vs. configured delay
- bench_process_info_resolution: Resolve ProcessInfo (command line, env, cwd) for 200 panes

### Property-Based Testing (proptest)
Add proptest cases in crates/wa-core/tests/proptest_process_relaunch.rs:
- **Process tree reconstruction**: For any arbitrary set of ProcessInfo entries (random shell types, random cwds, random agent types), the generated ProcessPlan must assign exactly one LaunchAction per pane, with no pane left unplanned. Strategy: arb_process_info_set() generates Vec of ProcessInfo with 1-100 entries.
- **Plan determinism**: For any ProcessInfo set and LaunchConfig, generating the plan twice must produce identical results (no non-determinism from HashMap iteration order, etc.).
- **Config-action consistency**: For any ProcessInfo, if launch_agents is false then no LaunchAgent actions may appear in the plan; if launch_shells is false then no LaunchShell actions may appear. Strategy: arb_launch_config() generates random boolean combinations.
- **Working directory validity**: For any LaunchShell or LaunchAgent action, the cwd must be a non-empty absolute path. Verify cwd.is_absolute() for all planned actions.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-09T19:32:46.886838Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-10T22:41:43.176747-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-10T22:41:43.176747-05:00","close_reason":"ProcessLauncher with plan/execute, LaunchConfig, agent detection, 21 tests. Committed in 394ff02.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-32z7","depends_on_id":"wa-rsaf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T19:35:07.749214Z","created_by":"jemanuel"},{"issue_id":"wa-32z7","depends_on_id":"bd-cuz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T19:35:29.500841Z","created_by":"jemanuel"},{"issue_id":"wa-32z7","depends_on_id":"wa-cuz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T19:35:29.500841Z","created_by":"jemanuel"},{"issue_id":"wa-32z7","depends_on_id":"wa-e6pq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T19:35:29.689184Z","created_by":"jemanuel"},{"issue_id":"wa-32z7","depends_on_id":"wa-1xcz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T19:35:29.8126Z","created_by":"jemanuel"}]}
